comment: Both denote a rigid classifier providing an identity/individuation principle to its instances (GFO: "immanent universals... exist in re"; UFO: "uniform principles of individuation, identity, and persistence"). GFO's `Universal` is not itself internally subdivided by rigidity/sortality the way UFO's `Kind` is positioned within a much larger typology — a difference in surrounding theoretical apparatus, not core definition, and a confidently-established one directly evidenced by each source's own text, not an open sourcing question.

Both denote a classifier that determines instance membership via some condition (SUMO: "an associated 'condition' that determines the instances"; GFO: "can be predicated of other entities"), but SUMO's `Class` is explicitly non-extensional and set-theoretically motivated, while GFO's `Universal` is explicitly a realist, immanent-in-particulars commitment — different enough in philosophical grounding that `overlap`, not `close-match`, is the more honest category.

object_id: *(no counterpart in any other of the 8 sources)*
comment: Recorded explicitly as a genuine SUMO-only category — no other source in this crosswalk distinguishes an extensional Set from an intensional Class the way SUMO's own `(partition SetOrClass Set Class)` does.
